title: calawaya exaltata
intro: Calawaya exaltata, also known as the "Peruvian Bark", is a plant native to the Andean region in South America, specifically the areas of modern-day Peru and Bolivia. The plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of quinine, a natural compound effective in treating malaria. The health benefits of Calawaya exaltata include reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of malaria, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to extract its medicinal properties, and in some cases, it is also used in herbal supplements and capsules.
